title: Maximum Product of the Length of Two Palindromic Subsequences
description:
Given a string s, find two disjoint palindromic subsequences of s such that the product of their lengths is maximized. The two subsequences are disjoint if they do not both pick a character at the same index.

Return _the maximum possible product of the lengths of the two palindromic subsequences_.

A subsequence is a string that can be derived from another string by deleting some or no characters without changing the order of the remaining characters. A string is palindromic if it reads the same forward and backward.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def stoneGameVII(stones):
    n = len(stones)
    dp = [[0] * n for _ in range(n)]

    for i in range(n - 1, -1, -1):
        for j in range(i + 1, n):
            dp[i][j] = max(stones[i] - dp[i + 1][j], stones[j] - dp[i][j - 1])

    return dp[0][n - 1]
```
    
    The solution uses dynamic programming. We consider the difference in score at any given range [i, j] in the stones array as the max of two possibilities:

1. The value of the leftmost stone (stones[i]) minus the difference in the remaining range [i + 1, j].
2. The value of the rightmost stone (stones[j]) minus the difference in the remaining range [i, j - 1].

We fill up the dp[i][j] matrix with the maximum difference for each range [i, j], starting from the bottom right and moving towards the top left. Once the entire dp matrix is filled, the value in dp[0][n - 1] represents the maximum score difference between Alice and Bob.

All implementations work with a time complexity of O(n^2) since we iterate through all pairs of (i, j) possible in the array.
